common.amountPositive: Amount must be greater than zero
common.dateBeforeBirth: Target date cannot be before birth date

age.metaTitle: Age Calculator — Calculate Your Exact Age
age.metaDescription: Enter your date of birth to instantly calculate your exact age in years, months, days, weeks, and total days lived.
age.title: Age Calculator
age.description: Calculate your exact age and time until your next birthday.
age.targetDateHint: Leave empty to use today's date
age.contentBody1: Calculating age involves subtracting your birth date from the current date or a specific target date. However, varying lengths of months (28, 29, 30, 31 days) and leap years can complicate this calculation.
age.contentBody2: Our calculator removes this complexity and provides your exact age in 'Years, Months, Days' format accurately. It also shows you how many total days you've lived, how many weeks have passed, and how many days are left until your next birthday.
age.contentBody3: You can use this tool when you need to state your exact age on a form, find out the exact age of a loved one, or track the age of a baby in months, weeks, or days.
age.formulaAge: Age = Target Date - Birth Date (Converted accurately)
age.formulaSource: Calculations strictly follow the standard Gregorian calendar.
age.faq1q: How is a baby's age in months calculated?
age.faq1a: For babies, month calculation is based on the day of birth. For example, a baby born on January 15th is considered exactly 1 month old on February 15th.
age.faq2q: How do leap years affect age calculation?
age.faq2a: Since our calculator uses Gregorian calendar standards, it automatically recognizes leap years and correctly factors February 29ths into the calculation.
age.faq3q: Can I find my age at a future date?
age.faq3a: Yes. By entering a future date in the 'Target Date' field, you can see exactly how old (years, months, days) you will be on that specific date.
age.faq4q: How many weeks are in a year?
age.faq4a: A standard year is 52 weeks and 1 day (2 days in leap years). Our 'Total Weeks' result is calculated by directly dividing the total number of days you've lived by 7.
age.faq5q: Is this age valid for astrological calculations?
age.faq5a: Astrology usually requires more detailed information like the exact time and location of birth. This calculator provides the Gregorian calendar age required for general and official purposes.

percentage.metaDescription: Perform percentage calculations instantly: find Y% of X, find what percentage X is of Y, or calculate the percentage change between two numbers.
percentage.title: Percentage Calculator
percentage.modeFindPercentage: A is what percent of B?
percentage.modeFindChange: Percentage change from A to B?
percentage.contentBody1: Percentage calculations are frequently used in daily life, whether calculating discounts while shopping, determining profit/loss margins in business, or analyzing data trends. Mathematically, a 'percentage' represents a fraction of 100 equal parts.
percentage.contentBody2: Our calculator offers three basic percentage operations on a single screen: finding a specific percentage of a number, finding what percentage one number is of another, and calculating the proportional change (increase or decrease) between two numbers.
percentage.contentBody3: You can use this tool to calculate discounted prices, understand interest rates, or evaluate your growth targets. All calculations are done instantly and locally on your device.
percentage.formulaFindPercentage: A is what % of B: (A / B) × 100
percentage.formulaFindChange: Change from A to B: ((B - A) / |A|) × 100
percentage.formulaSource: These formulas are based on universal mathematical principles.
percentage.faq1q: How to calculate a 20% discount on a product?
percentage.faq1a: First find 20% of the price (Price × 20 / 100), then subtract this value from the original price. Alternatively, you can multiply the price directly by 0.80.
percentage.faq2a: Subtract the old value from the new value. Divide the result by the old value and multiply by 100. A positive result indicates an increase, negative indicates a decrease.
percentage.faq3q: Can I use the percentage tool to calculate VAT?
percentage.faq3a: Yes, for example, if the VAT rate is 20%, you can use 'Increase from A to B' to add 20% to the net price, or use our dedicated VAT calculator.
percentage.faq4q: Can percentages be calculated from zero?
percentage.faq4a: Zero percent of any number is always zero. However, since division by zero is mathematically undefined, you cannot ask 'What percentage is A of 0?'.
percentage.faq5q: Can a percentage be greater than 100?
percentage.faq5a: Yes. For example, finding 150% of a value means finding 1.5 times that value. Increases greater than 100% mean the value has more than doubled.

idealWeight.metaTitle: Ideal Weight Calculator — Find Your Healthy Weight by Height
idealWeight.metaDescription: Instantly calculate your medically ideal weight and healthy weight range based on your height and gender.
idealWeight.title: Ideal Weight Calculator
idealWeight.description: Calculate the healthiest weight range for your body.
idealWeight.disclaimer: These results are based on standard medical formulas (Robinson, Miller, etc.). They may be misleading for athletes, pregnant women, or those with specific health conditions. Consult your doctor for a definitive target.
idealWeight.contentTitle: What is Ideal Weight and How is it Calculated?
idealWeight.contentBody1: Ideal weight is the body weight at which an individual is considered healthiest and has the lowest risk of chronic diseases, relative to their height, gender, and body frame. It is not just an aesthetic goal, but primarily a medical necessity.
idealWeight.contentBody2: Over the years, various formulas have been developed in the medical world to calculate ideal weight. The most well-known are the J.D. Robinson (1983), D.R. Miller (1983), and G.J. Hamwi (1964) formulas. Our calculator averages these universal formulas to give you the most accurate 'target' weight.
idealWeight.contentBody3: Ideal weight should be considered a 'range' rather than a single exact number. Your bone density and muscle mass determine where you fall within this range. Generally, weights that result in a Body Mass Index (BMI) between 18.5 and 24.9 are considered 'healthy'.
idealWeight.formulaRobinsonM: Robinson (Male): 52 kg + 1.9 kg per inch over 5 feet
idealWeight.formulaRobinsonF: Robinson (Female): 49 kg + 1.7 kg per inch over 5 feet
idealWeight.formulaBMI: Healthy Range: Between Height(m)² × 18.5 and Height(m)² × 24.9
idealWeight.formulaSource: Our calculator uses scientifically recognized international formulas.
idealWeight.faq1q: Does ideal weight change with age?
idealWeight.faq1a: Since height growth stops in adults (over 18), age does not have a large mathematical effect on ideal weight formulas. However, as muscle mass decreases and fat percentage may increase with age, staying near the upper limit of the healthy range is often considered normal.
idealWeight.faq2q: My calculated ideal weight seems too low/high, why?
idealWeight.faq2a: Standard formulas do not account for muscle mass and bone density (having a large or small frame). If you exercise heavily, your muscle mass will weigh more, making these results potentially unsuitable for you.
idealWeight.faq3q: Is Body Mass Index (BMI) the same as Ideal Weight?
idealWeight.faq3a: No. BMI is an index that tells you the ratio of your current weight to your height and places you into categories (underweight, normal, obese). Ideal weight is a specific weight or weight range target you should aim for.
idealWeight.faq4q: How does bone structure affect ideal weight?
idealWeight.faq4a: The healthy weight for large-framed individuals can be up to 10% more than small-framed individuals of the same height. This is why the healthy range is flexible.
idealWeight.faq5q: How can I reach my ideal weight?
idealWeight.faq5a: Through a healthy diet plan (caloric deficit or surplus) and regular exercise. You can use our 'Calorie Calculator' to find your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) and adjust your nutrition accordingly.

legal.comingSoonText: This page will be detailed soon.
legal.disclaimer: This calculator is for informational purposes only. Please consult an authorized institution for official matters.
legal.privacyContent: Hesapla prioritizes user privacy. This Privacy Policy outlines our commitment to data protection. Our application does not require user registration, and no personal data (name, email, IP address, location, etc.) is stored on our servers. All calculations are performed locally in your browser (client-side). Your records and favorites are stored only in your device's memory (IndexedDB); neither we nor any third party has access to this data.
legal.termsContent: By using Hesapla, you agree to the following terms: 1. All calculation tools provided are for informational purposes only and do not constitute medical, financial, or legal advice. 2. While we strive for accuracy, the developers cannot be held liable for any damages resulting from potential calculation errors. 3. Always consult authorized institutions or experts for official matters. 4. Unauthorized copying or commercial use of the application content is prohibited.
legal.cookiesContent: Hesapla only uses 'Local Storage' and 'IndexedDB' technologies necessary for basic application functions. These technologies store your preferences and calculation records on your device. When third-party services like Google AdSense or analytics tools are used, your explicit consent will be requested for their cookies.
legal.kvkkContent: Data Controller: Hesapla Team. Since we do not collect personal data, we have no registration obligations for personal data processing registries. Your records within the application are entirely under your control and can be deleted at any time by clearing your browser data. For any questions or requests, please contact us at support@hesapla.app.
legal.contactContent: For any questions, suggestions, or bug reports, you can reach us at: support@hesapla.app

vat.metaDescription: Add or remove VAT instantly: net to gross, gross to net. Supports 1%, 10%, 20% and custom rates. Free, fast, works offline.
vat.title: VAT Calculator
vat.disclaimer: This result is for informational purposes only. For official tax matters, please consult your local tax authority or a certified accountant.
vat.contentBody1: VAT (Value Added Tax) — called KDV in Turkish — is an indirect tax applied to the sale of goods and services in Turkey. As of 2024, the standard VAT rate is 20%. Reduced rates of 10% and 1% apply to selected categories.
vat.contentBody2: To add VAT to a net amount, multiply it by (1 + rate/100). To remove VAT from a gross amount, divide by (1 + rate/100). Our calculator handles this instantly — just enter the amount and rate.
vat.contentBody3: VAT calculation is essential for merchants, freelancers, and everyday shoppers. Use our tool for quick and accurate net/gross conversions.
vat.formulaSource: Source: GİB — VAT Law (Law No. 3065). This calculator is for informational purposes only.
vat.faq1q: How do I remove VAT from a VAT-inclusive price?
vat.faq1a: Divide the VAT-inclusive amount by (1 + VAT rate / 100). For 20% VAT, divide ₺1,200 by 1.20 to get a net amount of ₺1,000.
vat.faq2q: What is the standard VAT rate in Turkey?
vat.faq2a: As of 2024, the standard VAT rate in Turkey is 20%. Reduced rates of 10% and 1% apply to selected goods and services.
vat.faq3q: What is the difference between net and gross price?
vat.faq3a: Net price is the amount excluding VAT. Gross price is the final amount the buyer pays, including VAT.
vat.faq4q: What is the VAT calculation formula?
vat.faq4a: Add VAT: Gross = Net × (1 + rate/100). Remove VAT: Net = Gross / (1 + rate/100).
vat.faq5q: Is the VAT rate the same for all products?
vat.faq5a: No. In Turkey, rates of 1%, 10%, or 20% apply depending on the product or service category. Check the official GİB list for specific items.

calorie.metaTitle: Calorie Calculator — BMR and TDEE Estimation
calorie.metaDescription: Calculate your daily calorie needs, Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR), and BMI. Plan your calories for weight loss or gain goals.
calorie.title: Calorie Calculator
calorie.description: Calculate your body's daily energy requirements.
calorie.disclaimer: These results are estimates based on scientific formulas. Consult a nutritionist or doctor for personalized health advice and diet plans.
calorie.contentBody1: Your daily calorie requirement is the sum of the energy your body needs to maintain basic functions (BMR) and the energy needed for physical activity. Our calculator uses the Mifflin-St Jeor formula, widely considered the most accurate in modern science.
calorie.contentBody2: BMR (Basal Metabolic Rate) is the calories your body burns at rest just to keep you alive. TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) is your actual daily requirement, obtained by multiplying your BMR by your activity multiplier.
calorie.contentBody3: The golden rule of weight control: eat less than your TDEE to lose weight, and more than your TDEE to gain weight. For healthy weight loss, a daily deficit of 300-500 calories is generally recommended.
calorie.formulaSource: Source: Mifflin-St Jeor Equation (1990). This tool is not medical advice.
calorie.faq1a: BMR is the minimum amount of calories your body needs to maintain vital functions like breathing, blood circulation, and cell production while at rest.
calorie.faq2q: How reliable is BMI (Body Mass Index)?
calorie.faq2a: BMI is a general health indicator but doesn't account for muscle mass, bone density, or fat distribution. It may be misleading for athletes.
calorie.faq3q: How many calories should I eat to lose weight?
calorie.faq3a: Generally, consuming 500 calories less than your daily expenditure (TDEE) leads to about 0.5 kg of weight loss per week.
calorie.faq4q: How does activity level affect calorie needs?
calorie.faq4a: Calorie needs can vary by 1.5 to 2 times between a sedentary lifestyle and an extra active one.
calorie.faq5q: Does drinking water help burn calories?
calorie.faq5a: While it doesn't burn calories directly, drinking water supports metabolism and can help control calorie intake by promoting fullness.

salary.metaDescription: Calculate net from gross or gross from net salary in Turkey. See SGK, income tax, stamp duty and employer costs instantly. Updated for 2025.
salary.title: Salary Calculator
salary.description: Calculate your net and gross salary with 2025 Turkish deductions.
salary.note2025: 2025 rates: SGK 14%, unemployment 1%, stamp duty 0.759%. Minimum wage exemption applied.
salary.disclaimer: This calculation provides an estimate based on 2025 parameters. Income tax may differ in later months due to cumulative bracket calculation. Consult SGK or a certified accountant for exact figures.
salary.contentTitle: How Are Salary Deductions Calculated in Turkey?
salary.contentBody1: In Turkey, employees pay four main deductions on their gross salary: SGK employee share (14%), unemployment insurance employee share (1%), income tax, and stamp duty (0.759%). Since 2022, the income tax base up to the minimum wage is exempt — so employees earning near the minimum wage pay very little income tax.
salary.contentBody2: The 2025 income tax brackets are set annually: 15% up to ₺158,000; 20% for ₺158,001-330,000; 27% for ₺330,001-800,000; 35% for ₺800,001-4,300,000; 40% above that. Our calculator applies these brackets on a monthly basis.
salary.contentBody3: From the employer's perspective, the employer's SGK share (15.5%) and unemployment insurance share (2%) are added. So for an employee earning ₺30,000 gross, the employer's total cost is approximately ₺35,250.
salary.formulaNet: Net = Gross − SGK − Unemployment − Income Tax − Stamp Duty
salary.formulaSource: Source: SGK, GİB 2025 regulations. This tool is for informational purposes only.
salary.faq1q: What is the difference between net and gross salary in Turkey?
salary.faq1a: Gross salary is the total amount the employer agrees to pay. Net salary is what you actually receive after SGK, unemployment insurance, income tax, and stamp duty deductions.
salary.faq2q: What is the SGK employee contribution rate in 2025?
salary.faq2a: In 2025, the SGK employee contribution is 14% of gross salary. An additional 1% unemployment insurance brings total social security deductions to 15%.
salary.faq3q: Is the minimum wage exempt from income tax?
salary.faq3a: Yes. Since 2022, the income tax base corresponding to the minimum wage is exempt. Employees earning the minimum wage pay no income tax in 2025.
salary.faq4q: Why is stamp duty charged on salaries?
salary.faq4a: Stamp duty is charged at 0.759% of gross salary because the payroll document is considered a legal instrument. In 2025, the portion corresponding to the minimum wage is also exempt from stamp duty.
salary.faq5q: How much does the employer pay in SGK contributions?
salary.faq5a: The employer pays 15.5% of gross salary as SGK contribution and 2% as unemployment insurance. This means the employer pays roughly 17.5% more than the employee's gross salary.

knitting.metaTitle: Knitting Cost Calculator — Sales Price Calculation
knitting.metaDescription: Calculate the cost of your knitting and handmade projects. Add yarn, accessories, labor, and shipping costs to determine your sales price based on profit margin.
knitting.title: Knitting Cost Calculator
knitting.description: Calculate costs and sales prices for your handmade products.
knitting.disclaimer: This calculation reflects estimated costs. It is recommended to account for taxes and variable expenses separately.
knitting.contentBody1: Determining the value of a handmade product is more than just summing up material costs. For healthy pricing, you should include the cost of yarn, buttons, zippers, as well as the time you spend (labor) and extra expenses like shipping.
knitting.contentBody2: Our calculator works in two ways: it suggests a sales price based on your desired profit margin, or it shows how much profit you make from a set sales price.
knitting.contentBody3: For boutique producers, calculating labor based on an hourly rate equivalent to the minimum wage is a good starting point to find the true value of the product.
knitting.formulaTotal: Total Cost = Materials + Labor (Hours × Rate) + Shipping
knitting.formulaPrice: Sales Price = Total Cost × (1 + Profit Margin / 100)
knitting.formulaSource: This calculation is based on generally accepted cost principles for boutique producers.
knitting.faq1q: How should I determine my hourly labor rate?
knitting.faq1a: Consider your experience and the complexity of the task. For starters, you can base it on the hourly equivalent of the minimum wage.
knitting.faq2q: What expenses should I add as 'accessories'?
knitting.faq2a: You can add buttons, zippers, labels, packaging materials, and any secondary materials used for decoration in this section.
knitting.faq3q: What should the profit margin be?
knitting.faq3a: Generally, a profit margin between 30% and 100% is applied to boutique handmade products. Consider market conditions and your effort.
knitting.faq4q: Should I add shipping cost if the buyer pays for it?
knitting.faq4a: No. If shipping is paid by the buyer, you can leave this field blank. However, if you offer free shipping, you must include it.
knitting.faq5q: Is depreciation (needles, hooks, etc.) included in the cost?
knitting.faq5a: Since it's usually a very small amount, it's typically not included. However, if you use very expensive specialized equipment, you can add a small share to the 'accessories' section.

kitchenConverter.metaTitle: Kitchen Measurement Converter — Cups, Spoons, Liters
kitchenConverter.metaDescription: How many tablespoons in a cup? How many cups in a liter? Convert kitchen measurements instantly. Also shows gram equivalents per ingredient.
kitchenConverter.title: Kitchen Measurement Converter
kitchenConverter.description: Easily convert between cups, tablespoons, teaspoons, and liters.
kitchenConverter.fromUnitLabel: Which measurement do you want to convert?
kitchenConverter.showIngredients: Also show gram equivalent per ingredient
kitchenConverter.cheatSheetAlt: Kitchen measurement units illustrated comparison chart
kitchenConverter.contentBody1: The most common measurement unit in Turkish cuisine is the water glass, which equals 200 ml. The tea glass is half that: 100 ml. A tablespoon is 15 ml, a dessert spoon is 10 ml, and a teaspoon is 5 ml.
kitchenConverter.contentBody2: An important note: volume units do not correspond to the same gram weight for every ingredient. 1 cup of water weighs 200 g, but 1 cup of flour weighs about 120 g, and 1 cup of sugar weighs about 170 g. This difference is critical when converting recipes.
kitchenConverter.contentBody3: International recipes use 'cup' (1 cup = 240 ml), 'tablespoon' (≈15 ml), and 'teaspoon' (≈5 ml). These nearly match Turkish measurements, but note that the Turkish water glass is 200 ml, not 240 ml.
kitchenConverter.faq1a: The standard Turkish water glass is 200 ml. In international recipes, 'cup' equals 240 ml — note this difference when converting.
kitchenConverter.faq2q: How many grams is 1 cup of flour?
kitchenConverter.faq2a: 1 cup of flour is approximately 120 grams. Flour has a low density, so volume and weight differ significantly.
kitchenConverter.faq3q: How many grams is 1 cup of sugar?
kitchenConverter.faq3a: 1 cup of granulated sugar is approximately 170 grams. Powdered sugar is lighter — 1 cup equals about 112 grams.
kitchenConverter.faq4q: What is the difference between a tablespoon and a dessert spoon?
kitchenConverter.faq4a: A tablespoon (serving spoon) is 15 ml. A dessert spoon is smaller at 10 ml. A teaspoon is the smallest at 5 ml.
kitchenConverter.faq5a: 1 liter equals exactly 5 cups (since 1 liter = 1000 ml and 1 cup = 200 ml). Or 10 tea glasses.
